Two Traditions, One Story

Where Indigo Meets Batik

Chinese tie-dye captures the beauty of indigo through folds and stitches. Malaysian batik brings it to life with wax and dye.

Together, they create a world of patterns that speak across cultures and generations.

Discover More →

Chinese Tie-Dye

Shibori · Indigo · Harmony

Key Features

Uses folding, binding, and stitching to resist dye. Its signature look is deep indigo patterned against white cloth.

🪢Bind · Dip · Resist
📍Yunnan, China
Colours

Malaysian Batik

Wax · Dye · Heritage

Key Features

Uses hand-drawn wax lines to resist dye. Floral motifs glow in warm red, gold, blue, and white tones.

✒️Draw · Wax · Dye
📍Kelantan, Malaysia
Colours

Basic Knowledge

Understanding Tie-Dye & Batik

Both crafts use resist-based design, but they create different visual effects. Tie-dye is fluid and spontaneous; batik is ornamental and carefully outlined.

AspectChinese Tie-DyeMalaysian Batik
TechniqueResist-dyeing with tying, folding, stitching, or bindingWax-resist using canting, brush, or stamp
Pattern StyleOrganic, flowing, circular, and often unpredictableIntricate, controlled, floral, and symbolic
Common ColoursIndigo blue, white, soft natural tonesNavy, teal, orange, gold, warm tropical tones
Look & FeelSoft, meditative, fluid, handmadeDecorative, elegant, layered, timeless

Tools & Dyeing Materials

Tools & Dyeing Materials

This page includes the main tools and dyeing materials used in Chinese tie-dye and Malaysian batik.

Chinese Tie-Dye

Tools

  • Cotton or linen fabric
  • String, thread, rubber bands, wooden clips
  • Needles for stitched resist patterns
  • Dye vat, bucket, gloves, apron, drying rack

Dyeing Materials

  • Natural indigo dye
  • Synthetic fabric dye or fibre-reactive dye
  • Warm water and clean water basins
  • Salt, soda ash, or dye fixer, depending on dye type

Malaysian Batik

Tools

  • Cotton, rayon, or silk fabric
  • Canting tool for wax drawing
  • Batik block or stamp
  • Brushes, fabric frame, wax heater, gloves, apron

Dyeing Materials

  • Wax mixture for resist work
  • Fabric dyes
  • Fabric paints or liquid colourants
  • Sodium silicate or colour fixer, if used
  • Hot water or wax-removal materials for finishing
Making Process

From Resist to Pattern

Add real photos to each process step by clicking the image areas.

01

Prepare the Fabric

Wash, dry, and smooth the fabric before applying dye or wax.

02

Create the Resist

Fold and bind for tie-dye, or draw wax outlines for batik.

03

Apply Colour

Dip, brush, paint, or layer dye depending on the design.

04

Reveal the Pattern

Untie the fabric or remove wax to reveal the final pattern.

Interactive Mini Game

Tie-Dye or Batik? Guess the Craft

Click the correct answer for each material, tool, or pattern feature. This helps viewers remember the difference between Chinese tie-dye and Malaysian batik.

Score: 0/6
Answered: 0

Tied Circular Resist Rings

Created by tying fabric tightly before dyeing.

Canting Tool

Used to draw fine lines with hot wax.

Indigo Dye Vat

A dyeing container used for indigo resist-dyed textiles.

Wax-Resist Floral Motif

A floral batik-style motif created with wax-resist decoration.

String and Rubber Bands

Used to bind fabric and create tie-dye resist patterns.

Wax Mixture

A melted resist material used in batik before dyeing.
